repo.name: The Air Outside
repo.description: Personal art-project visual novel — Blazor WASM + Ink, 15 scenes, 9 endings

areas.STORY: Story engine — StoryService, Ink runtime, timeline, tags, state machine
areas.SAVE: Persistence — SaveService, SettingsService, localStorage keys and schema
areas.SETTINGS: User settings — defaults, ranges, reset, auto-advance, theme, audio volumes
areas.UI: UI shell — Stage, TopBar, panels, scene picker, fullscreen, responsive breakpoints
areas.CLIMAX: Climax pause mechanic — fade, dwell, auto-advance suppression, fourth-wall constraint
areas.AUDIO: Audio system — MusicService, procedural synth, mood-per-scene, no-files constraint
areas.NARR: Narrative content — scenes, gates, endings, first-person, em-dash, pacing tags
areas.BUILD: Build pipeline — InkBuild, WASM publish, Vercel deploy, zero-JS-deps constraint
areas.ART: Visual rendering — Background, Character, Motif, SpotArt SVG components

requirements.statement: Background.razor renders a non-empty SVG element when passed a recognized Background Id string; unrecognized ids fall back to a default (empty/sky) background without throwing.
requirements.why: Background.razor must render a non-empty SVG element for any background tag the Ink script emits; a missing background leaves the stage blank.

requirements.statement: Character.razor renders the protagonist_name Ink variable value as visible text on the character chest SVG when the character "wren" (or equivalent protagonist character) is on stage.
requirements.why: The player's chosen name must appear on the protagonist suit in the SVG so the character feels personalized.

requirements.statement: MusicService.StartAsync() is called on the first advance click after prologue completion; subsequent advances do not call StartAsync() again.
requirements.why: The Web Audio API requires a user gesture before starting; deferring start() to the first stage click satisfies the browser autoplay policy.

requirements.statement: MusicService.SetSceneMoodAsync(scene) is called whenever Story.Scene changes to a new value; it is not called again until the scene number changes.
requirements.why: Mood transitions on every scene change keep the ambient music synchronized with the narrative without requiring per-line audio tags.

requirements.statement: Every MusicService method wraps its JS interop call in a try/catch that swallows all exceptions; no exception propagates to the caller.
requirements.why: All audio calls are non-load-bearing; an audio subsystem failure must never block the story or crash the app.

requirements.statement: Running `dotnet run --project src/VisualNovel.InkBuild` exits with code 0 and writes a valid JSON file to src/VisualNovel.Shared/wwwroot/story.json.
requirements.why: The Ink compiler must run cleanly as a precondition for any gameplay; a compile failure is a blocking build error, not a runtime degradation.

requirements.statement: story.json parses as valid JSON and contains a top-level "inkVersion" integer field with a value greater than 0.
requirements.why: The Ink runtime requires a valid inkVersion field to load the story; a corrupt or empty file would cause a silent blank-screen failure.

requirements.statement: `dotnet publish src/VisualNovel.Web -c Release -o publish` exits with code 0 and produces publish/wwwroot/index.html.
requirements.why: The web publish target must be a fully self-contained static folder so it can be dropped on Vercel, Cloudflare Pages, or itch.io without a server.

requirements.statement: When IsClimaxPause is true, the background art, atmosphere, motion, motif, spot-art, and dialogue-wrap layers are not rendered; only the .climax div containing CurrentText is shown.
requirements.why: When IsClimaxPause is true the normal art/character/dialogue layers must be invisible so the player sees only the single invitational line against silence.

requirements.statement: ResetTimer() returns without starting a timer when IsClimaxPause is true, even if AutoAdvance is enabled.
requirements.why: The auto-advance timer must not fire at the climax; rapid-skipping behavior is deliberately broken here so the player must make a conscious click to proceed.

requirements.statement: A single click anywhere on the stage advances past the climax pause using the same AdvanceRequested() path as normal line advances; no dedicated climax-continue button exists.
requirements.why: The whole screen must be the advance affordance — no specific button, no "press SPACE" overlay. The click is the only required act.

requirements.statement: The .climax__line element fades in via the CSS animation "climax-fade-in 8s ease forwards", reaching a final opacity of 0.55.
requirements.why: The 8-second fade gives the text presence without demanding the player stop and stare; the half-opacity softens the instruction so it reads as an invitation.

requirements.statement: Before the climax pause renders, a ~3-second silence plays during which music stops and the UI transition is held; only after this silence does the .climax div appear and the ambient audio resume.
requirements.why: mechanics.md §Implementation note 1 specifies a pre-pause silence to make the transition felt as silence before the image appears, not as an abrupt UI change.

requirements.statement: SceneRegistry.Scenes contains exactly 15 entries with IDs 1–15, each with a unique Knot string and Title string.
requirements.why: SceneRegistry is the canonical scene list; any discrepancy between it and the Ink script would break the scene picker and StoryMap.

requirements.statement: GateOptions.ByGate contains exactly four gates ("gate1", "gate2", "gate3", "gate4") with 3, 2, 3, and 3 choices respectively, corresponding to SceneRegistry scenes 4, 5, 12, and 14.
requirements.why: GateOptions must match the gates in the Ink script; a mismatch would cause the StoryMap to display wrong gate labels.

requirements.statement: The compiled story produces exactly 9 distinct EndingTitle values across the gate3 × gate4 matrix, as defined in ink/scenes/16-epilogue.ink.
requirements.why: Nine distinct endings are a design commitment from CLAUDE.md; if two endings share a title the ending screen is misleading.

requirements.statement: The tag `pause: climax-window` appears in exactly one scene file (ink/scenes/13-step-outside.ink), once per gate3 branch (iris/stay/tae = 3 non-comment occurrences), so every playthrough path encounters the climax pause exactly once.
requirements.why: The `pause: climax-window` tag fires the climax mechanic; if it appears more than once, the mechanic breaks on every subsequent occurrence.

requirements.statement: No Ink source file under ink/ contains the pattern "Wren " followed by a verb in third-person singular form (e.g. "Wren notices", "Wren walks", "Wren says").
requirements.why: First-person narration is a Phase-6 design commitment; any third-person Wren reference would be a regression from the conversion pass.

requirements.statement: Progress is persisted to and loaded from localStorage under the exact key "the-air-outside.progress".
requirements.why: The localStorage key is a stable contract; changing it would lose all existing player saves.

requirements.statement: Settings are persisted to and loaded from localStorage under the exact key "the-air-outside.settings".
requirements.why: The settings key is a stable contract; changing it would reset every player's preferences on next launch.

requirements.statement: SaveProgress serializes exactly five fields: VisitedScenes (int[]), LastScene (int), LastUpdated (DateTime), PrologueDone (bool), and ProtagonistName (string, default "Wren").
requirements.why: The serialized shape is the on-disk contract; any field rename or type change is a breaking migration for existing saves.

requirements.statement: SaveAsync is called on every StoryService.StateChanged event after IsPrologueDone is true, persisting the current VisitedScenes, Scene, PrologueDone flag, and protagonist_name variable.
requirements.why: Progress must be auto-saved on every story state change so a browser close never loses more than one line of progress.

requirements.statement: ClearAsync() removes the progress key from localStorage and resets Current to a fresh SaveProgress with default values.
requirements.why: "Play Again" must produce a fully clean state; leftover visited scenes from a prior run would corrupt the StoryMap path highlight.

requirements.statement: AutoAdvance defaults to false; when true, the story advances automatically after AutoAdvanceDelay seconds if CanAdvance is true and no panel or pause menu is open.
requirements.why: Auto-advance must be off by default so first-time players read at their own pace without needing to disable it.

requirements.statement: AutoAdvanceDelay defaults to 3.5 seconds; the Settings UI exposes it as a range slider with min=1.0, max=10.0, step=0.5.
requirements.why: The slider range and step size must match the default so Reset restores to a valid position on the slider track.

requirements.statement: TextFadeMs defaults to 150; the Settings UI exposes it as a range slider with min=0, max=500, step=25.
requirements.why: Text fade creates the page-turn feel; 0ms disables it completely for players who prefer instant transitions.

requirements.statement: TextSize defaults to 1.0; accepts only the discrete values {0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0}; the Settings UI renders a 6-stop range slider (indices 0–5) mapping index to these values in order.
requirements.why: Six discrete stops prevent arbitrary fractional sizes that could break layout; the stop list is the canonical multiplier set.

requirements.statement: Theme defaults to "light"; accepts exactly "light" or "dark"; the Settings UI presents these as a segmented control with both options always visible.
requirements.why: Theme drives both the stage's CSS class and the UI ink color inversion; must default to light so the app looks correct out of the box.

requirements.statement: ReduceMotion defaults to false; when true, TransitionAdvance() calls Story.Advance() directly without the TextFadeMs delay.
requirements.why: Reduce motion accommodates vestibular sensitivity; when on, all CSS fade transitions are skipped and advance is instantaneous.

requirements.statement: MasterVolume defaults to 0.8, MusicVolume defaults to 0.7, SfxVolume defaults to 0.8; all three are range-bound 0.0–1.0.
requirements.why: Sensible volume defaults ensure the ambient music is audible without startling; UI-only until audio is wired.

requirements.statement: "Reset to defaults" sets AutoAdvance=false, AutoAdvanceDelay=3.5, TextFadeMs=150, TextSize=1.0, MasterVolume=0.8, MusicVolume=0.7, SfxVolume=0.8, Theme="light", ReduceMotion=false; save progress and VisitedScenes are not affected.
requirements.why: Reset must restore every setting to its initial value so players can recover from an accidental configuration without knowing the defaults.

requirements.statement: Every call to SettingsService.UpdateAsync() immediately serializes the current GameSettings to localStorage under the settings key.
requirements.why: Settings must survive a page refresh; persisting on every change is the simplest way to guarantee this without a "save settings" button.

requirements.statement: When Advance() is called and the Ink story canContinue, CurrentText is set to story.Continue() with leading and trailing whitespace trimmed.
requirements.why: The core narrative loop — each advance must fetch exactly one Ink line and strip whitespace so the UI never shows raw newlines.

requirements.statement: After each Advance(), CurrentChoices is populated from story.currentChoices; calling Choose(index) calls story.ChooseChoiceIndex(index) then Advance().
requirements.why: Choices must be surfaced immediately after Continue() so the stage can render choice buttons.

requirements.statement: GoBack() decrements the timeline index by one, restores the snapshot at that index, and returns true; when already at index 0 it returns false without modifying state.
requirements.why: Back navigation must restore the previous display state exactly — text, speaker, background, scene — so players can re-read without desync.

requirements.statement: When Advance() is called while _timelineIndex < _timeline.Count - 1 (i.e. replaying cached history), the snapshot at index+1 is restored instead of calling story.Continue().
requirements.why: Forward playback through the snapshot cache must replay stored state rather than re-running Ink, preserving deterministic history.

requirements.statement: JumpToKnot() clears Background, CharacterOnStage, Motif, Art, Audio, CurrentAudioCue, CurrentSfx, CurrentChoices, and the entire timeline before calling story.ChoosePathString(knot).
requirements.why: A scene jump must not carry over the previous scene's visual state; stale background/character/motif on a new knot would be a presentation bug.

requirements.statement: SetProtagonistName() coerces any null, empty, or whitespace-only input to "Wren" before writing the value to the protagonist_name Ink variable.
requirements.why: An empty name field in the prologue must not produce a nameless protagonist; "Wren" is the canonical default.

requirements.statement: When a tag `scene: N` (N an integer) is encountered, Scene is set to N and N is added to VisitedScenes.
requirements.why: VisitedScenes drives the scene picker's "visited" highlighting and the StoryMap's path trace.

requirements.statement: When a tag `pause: climax-window` is encountered during tag processing, IsClimaxPause is set to true.
requirements.why: IsClimaxPause gates the climax mechanic in Stage.razor — the tag is the handshake between Ink and the UI layer.

requirements.statement: When a tag `ending: <value>` is encountered, EndingTitle is set to the tag's value string.
requirements.why: EndingTitle drives the ending screen's title display; it must be captured from the Ink tag, not hardcoded.

requirements.statement: IsEnded is true when the story is loaded, CanAdvance is false, and IsAtChoice is false.
requirements.why: IsEnded must be computed correctly so Stage.razor knows when to show the ending screen vs dialogue.

requirements.statement: NameInput is rendered (and the main stage is hidden) when IsPrologueDone is false and Scene equals 0; once the prologue completes, the main stage becomes visible.
requirements.why: The prologue must gate the main stage so players set their name before the Ink protagonist_name variable is needed.

requirements.statement: A click on the stage div calls AdvanceRequested(); AdvanceRequested() is a no-op if IsPrologueDone is false, a panel is open, the pause menu is open, a pending jump modal is showing, a prologue replay is open, CanAdvance is false, or a transition is in progress.
requirements.why: Clicking the stage is the primary advance affordance; it must be blocked when any overlay is open to prevent accidental double-advances.

requirements.statement: When HandleJump() receives a scene with Id greater than Story.Scene, a confirmation modal is shown; the jump executes only after the player clicks "Jump anyway". Jumps to the current or an earlier scene execute immediately without a modal.
requirements.why: Forward jumps bypass unread scenes and their gate choices; the modal warns players that context will be missing without blocking them.

requirements.statement: The auto-advance timer is cancelled and not restarted while _openPanel is not Panel.None, _showPauseMenu is true, or IsClimaxPause is true.
requirements.why: Auto-advance must not fire while the player is reading the scene map, settings, or backlog — advancing behind a panel would be disorienting.

requirements.statement: When AutoAdvance is enabled and Story.Pacing equals "slow", the auto-advance delay is AutoAdvanceDelay × 1.7 seconds; all other pacing values use AutoAdvanceDelay directly.
requirements.why: Slow-pacing lines are motif moments designed to breathe; 1.7× gives them extra time under auto-advance without hard-blocking manual advance.

requirements.statement: When ReduceMotion is true or TextFadeMs equals 0, Story.Advance() is called directly without any delay; otherwise the stage waits TextFadeMs milliseconds before calling Advance() and updates the CSS transitioning class around the wait.
requirements.why: Text fade is a purely cosmetic transition; skipping it under ReduceMotion or when TextFadeMs=0 avoids any unnecessary delay for players who prefer immediacy.

requirements.statement: The stage root div carries CSS classes composed from: voice-{Voice}, pacing-{Pacing}, scene-{Scene}, background-{Background}, audio-{Audio}, theme-{Theme}, "reduce-motion" (when ReduceMotion), "climax-pause" (when IsClimaxPause), "bg-dark"/"bg-light", and "stage--menu-open" (when pause menu is open).
requirements.why: CSS class composition is the primary theming mechanism; all scene- and state-specific styling derives from these classes so no inline style overrides are needed.

requirements.statement: IsDarkBackground is true when Theme is "dark", or when Background is one of: "bedroom-night", "hallway-night", "taes-room", "field-night", "field-clearing", "nurse-office-night".
requirements.why: Dark-bg detection drives UI ink-color inversion so the TopBar and dialogue text remain readable over dark scene backgrounds.

requirements.statement: Fullscreen mode is toggled by pressing F or F11, and by clicking the fullscreen icon in the TopBar; in the web app this calls the DOM Fullscreen API, in the desktop app it sends a web-message via window.visualNovelHost.
requirements.why: Fullscreen must be togglable via keyboard so players can go borderless without touching the mouse.

requirements.statement: No audio files (mp3, ogg, wav, flac, m4a, aac, opus) exist anywhere under src/VisualNovel.Shared/wwwroot/ or src/VisualNovel.Web/wwwroot/.
requirements.why: A story about masks removing them must not itself hide behind AI-generated or pre-recorded assets; procedural generation is the only form of audio that ships.

requirements.statement: During the climax pause, only ambient/diegetic audio plays; no musical score or tonal swell is triggered.
requirements.why: mechanics.md specifies ambient/diegetic audio only at the climax — no score or string swell — to preserve the silence of the invitation.

requirements.statement: No node_modules directory exists under src/; no package.json with a non-empty "dependencies" field exists under src/.
requirements.why: Zero runtime JS dependencies is an architectural constraint; importing an npm package would break the no-bundle, static-file deploy model.

requirements.statement: The Vercel deployment (bash build.sh) produces a working SPA with: a catch-all rewrite routing all paths to index.html, a 1-year cache header on /_framework/ assets, and no-cache on index.html.
requirements.why: The Vercel deploy path is how players access the game publicly; a misconfigured SPA rewrite causes 404 on direct URL access.

requirements.statement: No minimum dwell time is enforced at the climax pause; the story advances to the next line immediately on any click regardless of how long the player waited.
requirements.why: No minimum dwell time preserves player autonomy; the pause is an invitation, not a timer gate. A 0.2s click and a 5-minute pause must produce the same next scene.

requirements.statement: No player-visible prose line in any Ink source file under ink/ contains an em-dash character (U+2014, —); Ink `//` comment lines are exempt.
requirements.why: Em-dashes were stripped in Phase 6; the auto-pause-comma pattern uses ellipses instead. Any em-dash reintroduced would violate the global style rule.

requirements.statement: The phrase "if you'd like" (or any variant of direct second-person address to the player) appears in the Ink source exactly once, in the climax scene (ink/scenes/13-step-outside.ink).
requirements.why: The climax line is the only direct address to the player; a second use would dilute its singularity and break the tonal restraint the story has maintained.

requirements.statement: The tag "pacing: slow" appears in exactly the following scene ink files: 01-cold-open, 03-dexterity-pe, 05-hallway, 07-crushed, 11-night-hallway, 13-step-outside, 15-coda.
requirements.why: Slow-default pacing on exactly the 7 motif-bearing scenes is the everyday-pacing mechanic from mechanics.md; it earns the climax pause by giving the player a rhythm to practice.

requirements.statement: Save and load failures (localStorage quota exceeded, unavailable, or parse error) are silently swallowed; the game continues running without displaying an error.
requirements.why: localStorage can be full or unavailable in private browsing; audio must not crash silently over a save failure.

requirements.statement: Each StorySnapshot record contains exactly 13 fields: StateJson, CurrentText, Speaker, Motif, Art, Background, CharacterOnStage, Voice, Pacing, Audio, Scene, IsClimaxPause, and EndingTitle.
requirements.why: The snapshot must capture all display state so back/forward navigation produces bit-identical UI reconstruction.

requirements.statement: The stage layout adapts at two breakpoints: 900px (tablet) and 600px (mobile); at each breakpoint font sizes and layout proportions reflow to maintain readability.
requirements.why: Responsive breakpoints ensure the game is playable on tablets and small laptops without horizontal scrolling.
